content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can align their memory compression and summarization policies to only retain long-term memory traces that spark new intrinsic curiosity, pruning low-curiosity experiences to maintain a compact, exploration-fostering long-term memory store. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent's compressed long-term memory continuously drives further useful exploration rather than becoming cluttered with trivial, uninformative experiences.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
